_purpose: Ground-truth dataset for validating the similarity engine AND the Day 53 resolver. Contains pre-computed normalized texts so the fixture can be used independently of the normalization pipeline.
description: Ahmed Hassan — 5 open historical commitments, 3 new statements from current standup. Mix of clear matches, clear non-matches, and one boundary case.
_owner_context: Ahmed Hassan has been in 4 previous standups. He has 5 open commitments tracked. Today's standup produced 3 new commitment statements that need to be resolved against the historical ones.

new_commitments.text: I'll finish the login feature today
new_commitments._normalization_note: 'feature' stems to 'featur' (len>4, ends 's'? No. len>3, ends 's'? No. len>4, ends 'ed'? No. len>5, ends 'ing'? No. stays 'feature'. Actually 'feature' -> f-e-a-t-u-r-e, 7 chars, ends in 'e', no suffix rule matches so stays 'feature'). Let's be accurate: normalize_text('I'll finish the login feature today') → lowercase → remove punct → 'ill finish the login feature today' → remove stopwords (ill, the) → ['finish','login','feature','today'] → stemming: finish(6>5,ends ing? no), login(5, ends ed? no, ends s? no), feature(7, ends ing? no, ends ed? no, ends s? no), today(5, ends ing? no, ends ed? no, ends s? no) → ['finish','login','feature','today'] → first 5 → 'finish login feature today'

new_commitments.text: I need to review the PR for the payment service
new_commitments._normalization_note: normalize_text → lowercase → 'i need to review the pr for the payment service' → remove punct → same → tokenize → ['i','need','to','review','the','pr','for','the','payment','service'] → remove stopwords (i, to, the, for, the) → ['need','review','pr','payment','service'] → stemming: need→need, review→review, pr→pr, payment→payment, service→service(ends 'e' not in rules) → first 5 → 'need review pr payment service'

new_commitments.text: I'll set up the CI/CD pipeline for staging
new_commitments._normalization_note: → 'ill set up the cicd pipeline for staging' → remove stopwords (ill, up, the, for) → ['set','cicd','pipeline','staging'] → no stemming → first 5 → 'set cicd pipeline staging'

historical_commitments.text: I'll finish the login feature by Thursday
historical_commitments._normalization_note: → 'ill finish the login feature by thursday' → remove stopwords (ill, the, by) → ['finish','login','feature','thursday'] → no stemming applies → first 5 → 'finish login feature thursday'

historical_commitments.text: I'll review the PR for the payment service by end of sprint
historical_commitments._normalization_note: → 'ill review the pr for the payment service by end of sprint' → remove stopwords (ill, the, for, the, by, of) → ['review','pr','payment','service','end','sprint'] → no stemming → first 5 → 'review pr payment service end'

historical_commitments.text: Write unit tests for the authentication module
historical_commitments._normalization_note: → 'write unit tests for the authentication module' → remove stopwords (for, the) → ['write','unit','tests','authentication','module'] → stemming: tests(ends s, len>3, not ss) → test, module(ends 'e' not in rules → 'module') → first 5 → 'write unit test authentication module'

historical_commitments.text: Schedule a demo call with the client
historical_commitments._normalization_note: → 'schedule a demo call with the client' → remove stopwords (a, with, the) → ['schedule','demo','call','client'] → no stemming rules apply → first 5 → 'schedule demo call client'

historical_commitments.text: Set up the CI/CD pipeline for the staging environment
historical_commitments._normalization_note: → 'set up the cicd pipeline for the staging environment' → remove stopwords (up, the, for, the) → ['set','cicd','pipeline','staging','environment'] → no stemming → first 5 → 'set cicd pipeline staging environment'

expected_matches._reason: Both normalize to 'finish login feature [today/thursday]' — 3/4 token overlap. High Jaccard and cosine. Core tokens (finish, login, feature) are identical.
'review pr payment service [need/end]' — 3 core tokens shared. Core reviewal of payment PR is the same commitment.
'set cicd pipeline [configuration/staging environment]' — 3 tokens shared (set, cicd, pipeline). Same CI/CD setup commitment.

expected_no_matches._reason: login feature vs PR review — no shared tokens
login feature vs unit tests auth — no shared tokens
login feature vs demo call client — no overlap
PR review payment vs unit tests — no shared tokens
PR review payment vs demo call client — no overlap
CI/CD pipeline vs login feature — no shared tokens
CI/CD pipeline vs PR review payment — no shared tokens
CI/CD pipeline vs demo call client — no overlap

boundary_case._desc: CI/CD pipeline setup vs unit tests for auth — partially similar verb context (setup vs write) but fundamentally different tasks. Score expected near 0.10-0.25. Not a match.
